article_title: Zhipu Releases GLM-5.2, a 744-Billion-Parameter Open Coding Model, Days After Washington Cut Off Foreign Access to Anthropic's Claude
summary: Substantively accurate; all specifics and quotes verify against the three cited sources. Approved with one clarification: the headline's unqualified 'Anthropic's Claude' could read as all Claude models, while the order covered only Fable 5 and Mythos 5.

content_preview.title: Zhipu Releases GLM-5.2, a 744-Billion-Parameter Open Coding Model, Days After Washington Cut Off Foreign Access to Anthropic's Claude
content_preview.summary: Zhipu's GLM-5.2 ships under an MIT license as a frontier coding alternative just after the US barred foreign nationals from Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Beijing-based Zhipu AI released GLM-5.2, a 744-billion-parameter open-weight model aimed at long-horizon coding, days after the US government cut off foreign access to Anthropic's flagship models. The Chinese lab announced the full release on Saturday and said the weights would be formally open-sourced under the permissive MIT license, [according to the South China Morning Post](https://www.scmp.com/tech/tech-trends/article/3357115/zhipu-ais-stock-rockets-after-chinese-firm-makes-gl...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece (660 words, within the 400-1200 News range). Clear Overview / What We Know / Export-Control Backdrop / What We Don't Know / Analysis structure. Technical depth on the model is appropriate and clearly attributed to the GLM-5 repository, which is correctly used as the primary source for all architecture and benchmark specifics (Rule 9).
editor_notes.source_verification: All three sources read from disk snapshots; all three uncompressed sha256 hashes match manifest.json. source-0 (raw.githubusercontent.com/zai-org/GLM-5 README, status 200): confirms verbatim '744 billion parameters (40B active)' / 'scales from 355B parameters (32B active) to 744B', '1M-token context that stably sustains long-horizon work', '23T to 28.5T tokens', DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA) 'largely reducing deployment cost while preserving long-context capacity', IndexShare 'reuses the same indexer across every four sparse attention layers, reducing per-token FLOPs by 2.9x at a 1M context length', SWE-bench Pro 62.1 and Terminal-Bench 2.1 81.0, and the SGLang/vLLM/Transformers/KTransformers deployment list. Note: in the README the 744B/DSA/23T-28.5T figures appear under the GLM-5 heading and IndexShare/1M under GLM-5.2; the download table confirms GLM-5.2 is itself 744B-A40B, so the article's attribution of the series architecture to GLM-5.2 holds. source-1 (SCMP, status 200): confirms MIT licence, GLM Coding Plan 'priced at just a tenth of Anthropic's premium Claude Code and Claude Max tiers', API 'scheduled to go live this week', stock 'surged as much as 48 per cent to HK$1,620 in morning trading and ended the day up 32.8 per cent at HK$1,457', 'soared on Monday', release announced 'on Saturday', and the Fable-5/Mythos-5 suspension quote. source-2 (MarkTechPost, status 200): confirms the June 12, 2026 order, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick's letter to CEO Dario Amodei, the bar on 'any foreign national, inside or outside the United States' including Anthropic's own foreign-national employees, the inability to filter foreign from US users in real time, and the 'few minor vulnerabilities' / 'other public models find the same flaws' characterization.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every specific (parameter counts, token counts, benchmark scores, FLOPs reduction, context length, stock figures, pricing ratio, order date, names) traces verbatim to a cited source. No hallucinated quotes. Dropped specifics from off-allowlist/403 sources (the '753B' variant, per-token API pricing, '$12.60/month', analyst ratings, specific coding-tool list) are genuinely absent from the body (Rule 2). VentureBeat/CNBC/Tom's Hardware are not cited. GLM-5.2, Zhipu, Anthropic, Claude, Fable 5, Mythos 5 all spelled verbatim (Rule 7).
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. The Analysis section frames the timing as a market opening but attributes the read to investors rather than editorializing. No AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. No prior GLM-5.2 / Zhipu stock coverage. Related but distinct: 2026-06/11 Anthropic Fable 5 launch and 2026-06/07 Commerce Department export-control guidance.
concerns: Headline scope: 'Days After Washington Cut Off Foreign Access to Anthropic's Claude' uses the unqualified 'Anthropic's Claude', whereas the cited MarkTechPost order applied only to Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5; other Claude models (Opus 4.8 and the rest) stayed online. The summary ('barred foreign nationals from Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5') and the body ('flagship models') are correctly scoped, so this is a headline-compression nuance, not a sourcing failure.

Minor attribution looseness: the article quotes the repo's GLM-5-series architecture description ('scales from 355B... to 744B...') while introducing it as 'the model' (GLM-5.2). The download table confirms GLM-5.2 is 744B-A40B, so the figure is correct for GLM-5.2; no correction needed.
recommendations: Future coverage could note that the Fable 5/Mythos 5 suspension was specific to those two models, not all of Claude, in the headline itself.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong, well-sourced News submission. The load-bearing geopolitical claim in the headline is solidly and directly sourced to MarkTechPost (order, date, scope, foreign-national bar all verify) and is correctly scoped in the summary and body. APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS to file a single public clarification that the order applied to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 specifically rather than to all Claude models, which the unqualified headline could imply.
